s_en: The Opening. A seven-verse prayer recited in every unit of salah, asking Allah for guidance to the straight path.
h_en: Revealed in Mecca, it is considered the essence of the Quran. The Prophet ﷺ said it is the greatest surah, and it serves as a preface to the entire Quran.
s_ms: Al-Fatihah. Doa tujuh ayat yang dibaca dalam setiap rakaat solat, memohon petunjuk Allah ke jalan yang lurus.
h_ms: Diturunkan di Makkah, ia dianggap intisari al-Quran. Nabi ﷺ bersabda ia adalah surah yang paling agung, dan ia menjadi pembuka kepada keseluruhan al-Quran.

s_en: The Cow. The longest surah, covering faith, law, guidance for family, commerce, and community life. It includes the famous Ayat al-Kursi.
h_en: Mostly revealed in Medina over several years. It addresses the early Muslim community's challenges, including dealings with the People of the Book and establishing Islamic law.
s_ms: Al-Baqarah. Surah terpanjang, merangkumi akidah, hukum, panduan keluarga, perniagaan, dan kehidupan masyarakat. Termasuk Ayat al-Kursi yang masyhur.
h_ms: Kebanyakannya diturunkan di Madinah selama beberapa tahun. Ia menangani cabaran masyarakat Islam awal, termasuk urusan dengan Ahli Kitab dan penetapan syariat Islam.

s_en: The Family of Imran. Discusses the oneness of Allah, the story of Maryam and Isa (Jesus), and the Battle of Uhud.
h_en: Revealed in Medina, partly in response to the Christian delegation of Najran who debated the Prophet ﷺ about the nature of Isa (Jesus).
s_ms: Ali Imran. Membincangkan keesaan Allah, kisah Maryam dan Isa a.s., serta Perang Uhud.
h_ms: Diturunkan di Madinah, sebahagiannya sebagai respons kepada delegasi Kristian Najran yang berbahas dengan Nabi ﷺ tentang hakikat Isa a.s.

s_en: The Women. Addresses women's rights, inheritance laws, marriage regulations, and social justice in the Muslim community.
h_en: Revealed in Medina after the Battle of Uhud, when many Muslim women were widowed and children orphaned, necessitating detailed social legislation.
s_ms: An-Nisa'. Membahas hak wanita, hukum pusaka, peraturan perkahwinan, dan keadilan sosial dalam masyarakat Islam.
h_ms: Diturunkan di Madinah selepas Perang Uhud, apabila ramai wanita Islam menjadi janda dan anak-anak menjadi yatim, memerlukan perundangan sosial yang terperinci.

s_en: The Table Spread. Completes Islamic law on food, oaths, and justice. Contains the finality of the Prophet's message.
h_en: One of the last surahs revealed in Medina. It includes the verse declaring the perfection of Islam as a religion, revealed during the Prophet's Farewell Pilgrimage.
s_ms: Al-Ma'idah. Melengkapkan syariat Islam mengenai makanan, sumpah, dan keadilan. Mengandungi ayat tentang kesempurnaan risalah Nabi.
h_ms: Antara surah terakhir yang diturunkan di Madinah. Termasuk ayat yang mengisytiharkan kesempurnaan Islam sebagai agama, diturunkan semasa Haji Wada' Nabi ﷺ.

s_en: The Cattle. A powerful Meccan surah affirming monotheism, refuting polytheism, and describing Allah's signs in nature.
h_en: Revealed in Mecca during a period of intense opposition. It addresses the Quraysh's accusations and their demand for miracles.
s_ms: Al-An'am. Surah Makkah yang kuat menegaskan tauhid, menolak syirik, dan menggambarkan tanda-tanda Allah di alam.
h_ms: Diturunkan di Makkah semasa penentangan yang sengit. Ia menjawab tuduhan Quraisy dan tuntutan mereka terhadap mukjizat.

s_en: The Heights. Narrates stories of earlier prophets (Adam, Nuh, Musa) and the consequences of rejecting divine guidance.
h_en: Revealed in Mecca. It was one of the surahs the Prophet ﷺ recited publicly, which moved some listeners to consider Islam.
s_ms: Al-A'raf. Menceritakan kisah para nabi terdahulu (Adam, Nuh, Musa) dan akibat menolak petunjuk ilahi.
h_ms: Diturunkan di Makkah. Ia antara surah yang Nabi ﷺ bacakan secara terbuka, yang menggerakkan hati sebahagian pendengar untuk mempertimbangkan Islam.

s_en: The Spoils of War. Lessons from the Battle of Badr, rules of warfare, and distribution of war gains.
h_en: Revealed in Medina shortly after the Battle of Badr (2 AH), addressing disputes among the Muslims over the distribution of war spoils.
s_ms: Al-Anfal. Pengajaran daripada Perang Badar, hukum peperangan, dan pembahagian harta rampasan perang.
h_ms: Diturunkan di Madinah sejurus selepas Perang Badar (2 H), menangani perselisihan dalam kalangan orang Islam mengenai pembahagian ghanimah.

s_en: The Repentance. The only surah without Bismillah. Addresses hypocrites, treaty violations, and the call to sincere repentance.
h_en: Revealed in Medina around 9 AH after the Treaty of Hudaybiyyah was violated. It is among the last surahs revealed and deals with the Tabuk expedition.
s_ms: At-Taubah. Satu-satunya surah tanpa Bismillah. Membahas golongan munafik, pelanggaran perjanjian, dan seruan kepada taubat yang ikhlas.
h_ms: Diturunkan di Madinah sekitar 9 H selepas Perjanjian Hudaibiyah dilanggar. Ia antara surah terakhir yang diturunkan dan menyentuh ekspedisi Tabuk.

s_en: Jonah. Affirms prophethood through the story of Yunus and other prophets, emphasizing Allah's mercy and patience.
h_en: Revealed in Mecca. Named after Prophet Yunus whose story of being swallowed by the whale illustrates Allah's mercy upon sincere repentance.
s_ms: Yunus. Menegaskan kenabian melalui kisah Yunus dan nabi-nabi lain, menekankan rahmat dan kesabaran Allah.
h_ms: Diturunkan di Makkah. Dinamakan sempena Nabi Yunus yang kisahnya ditelan ikan paus menggambarkan rahmat Allah atas taubat yang ikhlas.

s_en: Hud. Continues the theme of prophetic stories — Nuh, Hud, Salih, Ibrahim, Lut, and others — as warnings to disbelievers.
h_en: Revealed in Mecca. The Prophet ﷺ said this surah and its sisters (Al-Waqi'ah, Al-Mursalat, An-Naba, At-Takwir) aged him due to their vivid descriptions of Judgment Day.
s_ms: Hud. Meneruskan tema kisah kenabian — Nuh, Hud, Soleh, Ibrahim, Lut, dan lain-lain — sebagai amaran kepada orang kafir.
h_ms: Diturunkan di Makkah. Nabi ﷺ bersabda surah ini dan surah-surah saudaranya (Al-Waqi'ah, Al-Mursalat, An-Naba, At-Takwir) menjadikan Baginda tua kerana gambaran Hari Kiamat yang vivid.

s_en: Joseph. The complete, beautifully narrated story of Prophet Yusuf — from his childhood dream to his rise in Egypt.
h_en: Revealed in Mecca during the Year of Sorrow, comforting the Prophet ﷺ by paralleling his trials with those of Yusuf, who was also rejected by his own people.
s_ms: Yusuf. Kisah lengkap dan indah Nabi Yusuf — dari mimpi kanak-kanak hingga kebangkitannya di Mesir.
h_ms: Diturunkan di Makkah semasa Tahun Kesedihan, menghiburkan Nabi ﷺ dengan menyamakan ujian Baginda dengan ujian Yusuf yang turut ditolak oleh kaumnya.

s_en: The Thunder. Discusses Allah's signs in nature, the truth of revelation, and the contrast between belief and disbelief.
h_en: Revealed in Medina. Named after the verse that describes thunder as glorifying Allah, blending natural phenomena with spiritual reflection.
s_ms: Ar-Ra'd. Membincangkan tanda-tanda Allah di alam, kebenaran wahyu, dan perbezaan antara iman dan kekafiran.
h_ms: Diturunkan di Madinah. Dinamakan sempena ayat yang menggambarkan guruh sebagai bertasbih kepada Allah, menggabungkan fenomena alam dengan renungan rohani.

s_en: Abraham. Centers on Prophet Ibrahim's prayers for Makkah and his descendants, and the parable of the good and bad tree.
h_en: Revealed in Mecca. Contains Ibrahim's famous supplication for the city of Makkah and his progeny, linking the Hajj pilgrimage to monotheism.
s_ms: Ibrahim. Berpusat pada doa Nabi Ibrahim untuk Makkah dan keturunannya, serta perumpamaan pokok yang baik dan buruk.
h_ms: Diturunkan di Makkah. Mengandungi doa masyhur Ibrahim untuk kota Makkah dan zuriatnya, mengaitkan ibadah haji dengan tauhid.

s_en: The Rocky Tract. Describes the creation, the punishment of past nations, and the preservation of the Quran from corruption.
h_en: Revealed in Mecca. Contains Allah's promise to preserve the Quran, and narrates the story of the People of the Cave-like rocky tract (Al-Hijr).
s_ms: Al-Hijr. Menerangkan penciptaan, azab umat terdahulu, dan pemeliharaan al-Quran daripada penyelewengan.
h_ms: Diturunkan di Makkah. Mengandungi janji Allah untuk memelihara al-Quran, dan menceritakan kisah penduduk Al-Hijr.

s_en: The Bee. Celebrates Allah's blessings in nature — the bee, mountains, seas — and calls humanity to gratitude.
h_en: Revealed in Mecca. Named after the verse about the bee's divinely inspired behavior, illustrating Allah's wisdom in even the smallest creatures.
s_ms: An-Nahl. Memuji nikmat Allah di alam — lebah, gunung, lautan — dan menyeru manusia untuk bersyukur.
h_ms: Diturunkan di Makkah. Dinamakan sempena ayat tentang tingkah laku lebah yang diilhamkan oleh Allah, menggambarkan kebijaksanaan Allah dalam makhluk sekecil mana pun.

s_en: The Night Journey. Opens with the miraculous Isra (night journey) of the Prophet ﷺ from Makkah to Jerusalem.
h_en: Revealed in Mecca around 621 CE, shortly after the Prophet's miraculous night journey (Isra) to Jerusalem and ascension (Mi'raj).
s_ms: Al-Isra'. Bermula dengan Isra' mukjizat Nabi ﷺ dari Makkah ke Baitulmaqdis.
h_ms: Diturunkan di Makkah sekitar 621 M, sejurus selepas perjalanan malam mukjizat Nabi ﷺ (Isra') ke Baitulmaqdis dan Mi'raj.

s_en: The Cave. Contains four powerful stories: the People of the Cave, the Man of Two Gardens, Musa and Khidr, and Dhul-Qarnayn.
h_en: Revealed in Mecca in response to questions posed by the Quraysh (prompted by Jewish scholars) to test the Prophet's ﷺ prophethood.
s_ms: Al-Kahfi. Mengandungi empat kisah hebat: Ashabul Kahfi, Pemilik Dua Kebun, Musa dan Khidir, serta Zulkarnain.
h_ms: Diturunkan di Makkah sebagai jawapan kepada soalan Quraisy (yang dicadangkan oleh ulama Yahudi) untuk menguji kenabian Nabi ﷺ.

s_en: Mary. Tells the miraculous birth of Isa (Jesus) and Yahya (John), and the story of Maryam, the most honored woman in Islam.
h_en: Revealed in Mecca. Ja'far ibn Abi Talib recited it before the Negus of Abyssinia, moving the Christian king to tears and securing protection for Muslim refugees.
s_ms: Maryam. Menceritakan kelahiran mukjizat Isa a.s. dan Yahya a.s., serta kisah Maryam, wanita paling dimuliakan dalam Islam.
h_ms: Diturunkan di Makkah. Ja'far bin Abi Talib membacanya di hadapan Raja Habsyah, menggerakkan raja Kristian itu hingga menangis dan memberikan perlindungan kepada pelarian Islam.

s_en: Ta-Ha. Opens with consoling letters, then narrates the full story of Musa — his mission, confrontation with Pharaoh, and the Exodus.
h_en: Revealed in Mecca. It is said that Umar ibn al-Khattab embraced Islam after hearing this surah recited at his sister's house.
s_ms: Taha. Bermula dengan huruf-huruf penghiburan, kemudian menceritakan kisah lengkap Musa — misi, pertembungan dengan Firaun, dan Eksodus.
h_ms: Diturunkan di Makkah. Dikatakan Umar bin Al-Khattab memeluk Islam selepas mendengar surah ini dibaca di rumah adiknya.

s_en: The Prophets. Recounts stories of multiple prophets (Ibrahim, Nuh, Dawud, Sulayman, Ayyub, Yunus) emphasizing Allah's mercy.
h_en: Revealed in Mecca. It highlights that all prophets brought the same core message of monotheism and that Allah's mercy encompasses all things.
s_ms: Al-Anbiya'. Menceritakan kisah pelbagai nabi (Ibrahim, Nuh, Daud, Sulaiman, Ayyub, Yunus) menekankan rahmat Allah.
h_ms: Diturunkan di Makkah. Ia menekankan bahawa semua nabi membawa mesej tauhid yang sama dan rahmat Allah merangkumi segala-galanya.

s_en: The Pilgrimage. Unique in combining Meccan and Madinan themes — vivid Judgment Day imagery alongside Hajj legislation.
h_en: Revealed partly in Mecca and partly in Medina. It establishes the rites of Hajj and the sanctity of the Ka'bah.
s_ms: Al-Hajj. Unik kerana menggabungkan tema Makkah dan Madinah — gambaran Hari Kiamat berserta perundangan haji.
h_ms: Diturunkan sebahagiannya di Makkah dan sebahagiannya di Madinah. Ia menetapkan rukun haji dan kesucian Ka'bah.

s_en: The Believers. Describes the qualities of successful believers and narrates the stories of Nuh and Musa as warnings.
h_en: Revealed in Mecca. It outlines the attributes of true believers who will inherit Paradise, contrasting them with those lost in worldly pursuits.
s_ms: Al-Mu'minun. Menggambarkan sifat-sifat orang beriman yang berjaya dan menceritakan kisah Nuh dan Musa sebagai amaran.
h_ms: Diturunkan di Makkah. Ia menggariskan sifat orang beriman sejati yang akan mewarisi Syurga, membezakan mereka dengan yang tenggelam dalam urusan dunia.

s_en: The Light. Contains the famous Light Verse (Ayat an-Nur), laws on modesty, chastity, and the incident of Ifk (slander against Aisha).
h_en: Revealed in Medina around 6 AH, largely in response to the slander incident against Aisha (the Prophet's wife), establishing laws on accusation and modesty.
s_ms: An-Nur. Mengandungi Ayat Nur yang masyhur, hukum tentang kesopanan, kesucian, dan peristiwa Ifk (fitnah terhadap Aisyah).
h_ms: Diturunkan di Madinah sekitar 6 H, sebahagian besarnya sebagai respons kepada peristiwa fitnah terhadap Aisyah (isteri Nabi), menetapkan hukum tentang tuduhan dan kesopanan.

s_en: The Criterion. Distinguishes truth from falsehood through Allah's signs and the example of the righteous servant.
h_en: Revealed in Mecca. Named 'Al-Furqan' (The Criterion) because the Quran itself is the criterion between right and wrong.
s_ms: Al-Furqan. Membezakan kebenaran daripada kebatilan melalui tanda-tanda Allah dan contoh hamba yang soleh.
h_ms: Diturunkan di Makkah. Dinamakan 'Al-Furqan' (Pembeza) kerana al-Quran itu sendiri adalah pembeza antara yang hak dan yang batil.

s_en: The Poets. Narrates the stories of Musa, Ibrahim, Nuh, Hud, Salih, Lut, and Shu'ayb, all rejecting idolatry.
h_en: Revealed in Mecca. Responds to the Quraysh poets who mocked the Prophet ﷺ, clarifying that true poetry and divine revelation are vastly different.
s_ms: Asy-Syu'ara'. Menceritakan kisah Musa, Ibrahim, Nuh, Hud, Soleh, Lut, dan Syu'aib, semua menolak penyembahan berhala.
h_ms: Diturunkan di Makkah. Menjawab penyair Quraisy yang mengejek Nabi ﷺ, menjelaskan bahawa puisi dan wahyu ilahi sangat berbeza.

s_en: The Ant. Features the story of Sulayman and the ant, the Queen of Sheba, and the theme of gratitude for divine blessings.
h_en: Revealed in Mecca. Named after the verse where an ant warns others to hide from Sulayman's marching army, illustrating Allah's care for all creation.
s_ms: An-Naml. Memaparkan kisah Sulaiman dan semut, Ratu Saba, dan tema kesyukuran atas nikmat Allah.
h_ms: Diturunkan di Makkah. Dinamakan sempena ayat di mana seekor semut memberi amaran kepada yang lain supaya bersembunyi daripada tentera Sulaiman, menggambarkan kasih Allah kepada semua makhluk.

s_en: The Stories. Details the life of Musa from birth to prophethood, and the story of Qarun (Korah) and his wealth.
h_en: Revealed in Mecca. Parallels the Prophet's ﷺ own experience of exile and return, using Musa's story as comfort and reassurance.
s_ms: Al-Qasas. Memperincikan kehidupan Musa dari lahir hingga kenabian, dan kisah Qarun dengan kekayaannya.
h_ms: Diturunkan di Makkah. Menyamakan pengalaman Nabi ﷺ sendiri dalam pembuangan dan kepulangan, menggunakan kisah Musa sebagai penghiburan.

s_en: The Spider. Tests of faith, the parable of the spider's fragile web, and Allah's signs in creation.
h_en: Revealed in Mecca. Named after the parable that those who take protectors besides Allah are like a spider building the weakest of houses.
s_ms: Al-Ankabut. Ujian iman, perumpamaan sarang labah-labah yang rapuh, dan tanda-tanda Allah dalam penciptaan.
h_ms: Diturunkan di Makkah. Dinamakan sempena perumpamaan bahawa mereka yang mengambil pelindung selain Allah adalah seperti labah-labah yang membina rumah yang paling lemah.

s_en: The Romans. Prophecies the Roman victory over the Persians and reflects on Allah's signs in creation and history.
h_en: Revealed in Mecca around 615 CE when the Byzantines suffered defeat. It boldly predicted their victory within 3-9 years, which came true.
s_ms: Ar-Rum. Meramalkan kemenangan Rom ke atas Parsi dan merenung tanda-tanda Allah dalam penciptaan dan sejarah.
h_ms: Diturunkan di Makkah sekitar 615 M apabila Byzantium mengalami kekalahan. Ia dengan berani meramalkan kemenangan mereka dalam 3-9 tahun, yang menjadi kenyataan.

s_en: Luqman. Features the wise counsel of Luqman to his son — establishing prayer, enjoining good, and humility.
h_en: Revealed in Mecca. Luqman was a wise man (not a prophet) whose advice to his son became a timeless model of parental guidance in Islam.
s_ms: Luqman. Memaparkan nasihat bijak Luqman kepada anaknya — mendirikan solat, menyuruh kebaikan, dan bersikap rendah hati.
h_ms: Diturunkan di Makkah. Luqman adalah seorang yang bijak (bukan nabi) yang nasihatnya kepada anaknya menjadi model panduan ibu bapa yang abadi dalam Islam.

s_en: The Prostration. Describes the Day of Judgment and the believers who prostrate in worship, contrasting them with the arrogant.
h_en: Revealed in Mecca. The Prophet ﷺ used to recite it on Friday mornings, and it contains a verse of prostration (sajdah).
s_ms: As-Sajdah. Menggambarkan Hari Kiamat dan orang beriman yang sujud dalam ibadah, membezakan mereka dengan yang sombong.
h_ms: Diturunkan di Makkah. Nabi ﷺ biasa membacanya pada pagi Jumaat, dan ia mengandungi ayat sujud.

s_en: The Confederates. Covers the Battle of the Trench, the Prophet's household etiquette, and his status as the final Messenger.
h_en: Revealed in Medina around 5 AH after the Battle of the Trench (Ahzab). Also addresses the Prophet's marriages and the hijab ruling.
s_ms: Al-Ahzab. Merangkumi Perang Khandaq, adab rumah tangga Nabi, dan kedudukannya sebagai Rasul terakhir.
h_ms: Diturunkan di Madinah sekitar 5 H selepas Perang Khandaq (Ahzab). Juga menyentuh perkahwinan Nabi dan hukum hijab.

s_en: Sheba. Tells the story of the people of Saba (Sheba), their blessings, ingratitude, and subsequent punishment.
h_en: Revealed in Mecca. Warns against the arrogance that comes with prosperity, using the example of the ancient kingdom of Sheba in Yemen.
s_ms: Saba'. Menceritakan kisah kaum Saba', nikmat mereka, keingkaran, dan azab yang menyusul.
h_ms: Diturunkan di Makkah. Memberi amaran terhadap kesombongan yang datang dengan kemakmuran, menggunakan contoh kerajaan purba Saba' di Yaman.

s_en: The Originator. Celebrates Allah as Creator and Originator, describing angels and the wonders of creation.
h_en: Revealed in Mecca. Named after the attribute of Allah as 'Fatir' (Originator) of the heavens and earth, emphasizing divine creativity.
s_ms: Fatir. Memuji Allah sebagai Pencipta dan Pengasas, menggambarkan malaikat dan keajaiban penciptaan.
h_ms: Diturunkan di Makkah. Dinamakan sempena sifat Allah sebagai 'Fatir' (Pencipta) langit dan bumi, menekankan kreativiti ilahi.

s_en: Ya-Sin. Called the 'heart of the Quran.' Covers resurrection, prophetic narratives, and Allah's signs in nature.
h_en: Revealed in Mecca. The Prophet ﷺ called it the heart of the Quran. It is widely recited for the dying and in times of need.
s_ms: Yasin. Digelar 'jantung al-Quran.' Merangkumi kebangkitan, kisah kenabian, dan tanda-tanda Allah di alam.
h_ms: Diturunkan di Makkah. Nabi ﷺ menggelarnya jantung al-Quran. Ia sering dibaca untuk orang yang nazak dan dalam masa keperluan.

s_en: Those Ranged in Ranks. Describes angels in ranks, the sacrifice of Ibrahim's son, and the ultimate triumph of Allah's messengers.
h_en: Revealed in Mecca. Contains the profound story of Ibrahim being commanded to sacrifice his son, demonstrating ultimate submission to Allah.
s_ms: As-Saffat. Menggambarkan malaikat berbaris, pengorbanan anak Ibrahim, dan kemenangan muktamad rasul-rasul Allah.
h_ms: Diturunkan di Makkah. Mengandungi kisah mendalam Ibrahim diperintahkan mengorbankan anaknya, menunjukkan kepatuhan mutlak kepada Allah.

s_en: Sad. Narrates the stories of Dawud, Sulayman, and Ayyub — prophets tested with power, wealth, and suffering.
h_en: Revealed in Mecca. Opens with the letter 'Sad' and addresses the Quraysh's astonishment that a man from among them claims prophethood.
s_ms: Sad. Menceritakan kisah Daud, Sulaiman, dan Ayyub — nabi-nabi yang diuji dengan kuasa, kekayaan, dan penderitaan.
h_ms: Diturunkan di Makkah. Bermula dengan huruf 'Sad' dan menjawab kehairanan Quraisy bahawa seorang dari kalangan mereka mendakwa kenabian.

s_en: The Groups. Discusses sincerity in worship, the parable of the neighbor, and groups entering Paradise and Hellfire.
h_en: Revealed in Mecca. Emphasizes that true religion is sincere devotion to Allah alone, warning against showing off in worship.
s_ms: Az-Zumar. Membincangkan keikhlasan dalam ibadah, perumpamaan jiran, dan kumpulan-kumpulan yang memasuki Syurga dan Neraka.
h_ms: Diturunkan di Makkah. Menekankan bahawa agama yang benar adalah pengabdian ikhlas kepada Allah semata-mata, memberi amaran terhadap riak dalam ibadah.

s_en: The Forgiver. Opens with Allah's attributes of forgiveness, narrates the story of a believer from Pharaoh's people.
h_en: Revealed in Mecca. Also called 'Al-Mu'min' (The Believer) for the story of the man who secretly believed in Musa from among Pharaoh's court.
s_ms: Ghafir. Bermula dengan sifat keampunan Allah, menceritakan kisah seorang yang beriman dari kalangan kaum Firaun.
h_ms: Diturunkan di Makkah. Juga dipanggil 'Al-Mu'min' (Orang Beriman) kerana kisah lelaki yang beriman secara rahsia dengan Musa dari kalangan istana Firaun.

s_en: Explained in Detail. The Quran is explained in detail as guidance, describing the creation and human response to trials.
h_en: Revealed in Mecca. Named for its detailed exposition of Allah's signs, the creation of the heavens and earth, and human ingratitude.
s_ms: Fussilat. Al-Quran dijelaskan secara terperinci sebagai petunjuk, menerangkan penciptaan dan tindak balas manusia terhadap ujian.
h_ms: Diturunkan di Makkah. Dinamakan kerana penjelasan terperinci tentang tanda-tanda Allah, penciptaan langit dan bumi, dan ketidakbersyukuran manusia.

s_en: Consultation. Emphasizes mutual consultation (shura) in community affairs and the unity of the prophetic message.
h_en: Revealed in Mecca. Establishes the principle of shura (consultation) as a fundamental Islamic governance and community value.
s_ms: Asy-Syura. Menekankan musyawarah dalam urusan masyarakat dan kesatuan risalah kenabian.
h_ms: Diturunkan di Makkah. Menetapkan prinsip syura (permesyuaratan) sebagai nilai asas tadbir urus dan masyarakat Islam.

s_en: Ornaments of Gold. Challenges materialism and the idolaters' claim that angels are Allah's daughters.
h_en: Revealed in Mecca. Critiques the human obsession with gold and worldly ornaments, redirecting attention to eternal spiritual values.
s_ms: Az-Zukhruf. Mencabar materialisme dan dakwaan musyrikin bahawa malaikat adalah anak perempuan Allah.
h_ms: Diturunkan di Makkah. Mengkritik obsesi manusia terhadap emas dan perhiasan dunia, mengalihkan perhatian kepada nilai rohani yang kekal.

s_en: The Smoke. Warns of a coming smoke (punishment) and narrates the story of Pharaoh's destruction.
h_en: Revealed in Mecca. Refers to a famine-induced haze that struck the Quraysh, seen as a sign of impending divine punishment.
s_ms: Ad-Dukhan. Memberi amaran tentang asap (azab) yang akan datang dan menceritakan kisah kebinasaan Firaun.
h_ms: Diturunkan di Makkah. Merujuk kepada jerebu akibat kebuluran yang menimpa Quraisy, dilihat sebagai tanda azab ilahi yang akan datang.

s_en: The Kneeling. Describes people kneeling on Judgment Day and the consequences of denying Allah's signs.
h_en: Revealed in Mecca. Vividly portrays the Day of Judgment when every community will be seen kneeling in anticipation of their reckoning.
s_ms: Al-Jasiyah. Menggambarkan orang-orang berlutut pada Hari Kiamat dan akibat mengingkari tanda-tanda Allah.
h_ms: Diturunkan di Makkah. Menggambarkan dengan jelas Hari Kiamat apabila setiap umat dilihat berlutut menanti perhitungan mereka.

s_en: The Dunes. Addresses the jinn who listened to the Quran, the people of 'Ad, and the importance of responding to divine calls.
h_en: Revealed in Mecca. Named after the sand dunes where a group of jinn gathered to listen to the Prophet's ﷺ recitation.
s_ms: Al-Ahqaf. Membahas jin yang mendengar al-Quran, kaum 'Ad, dan kepentingan menyahut seruan ilahi.
h_ms: Diturunkan di Makkah. Dinamakan sempena bukit pasir di mana sekumpulan jin berkumpul mendengar bacaan Nabi ﷺ.

s_en: Muhammad. Rules of warfare, the treatment of prisoners, and the hypocrisy of those who claim faith without action.
h_en: Revealed in Medina. Addresses the Battle of Uhud aftermath and the behavior of hypocrites who undermined the Muslim community.
s_ms: Muhammad. Hukum peperangan, layanan terhadap tawanan, dan kemunafikan mereka yang mendakwa beriman tanpa amal.
h_ms: Diturunkan di Madinah. Menangani kesan Perang Uhud dan kelakuan golongan munafik yang melemahkan masyarakat Islam.

s_en: The Victory. Celebrates the Treaty of Hudaybiyyah as a clear victory and foretells future conquests.
h_en: Revealed in Medina around 6 AH during the return from Hudaybiyyah. The treaty, seemingly unfavorable, proved to be a strategic and spiritual triumph.
s_ms: Al-Fath. Merayakan Perjanjian Hudaibiyah sebagai kemenangan yang jelas dan meramalkan penaklukan masa depan.
h_ms: Diturunkan di Madinah sekitar 6 H semasa kepulangan dari Hudaibiyah. Perjanjian yang nampaknya tidak menguntungkan itu terbukti sebagai kemenangan strategik dan rohani.

s_en: The Rooms. Etiquette in the Prophet's household, the brotherhood of believers, and the dangers of mockery and backbiting.
h_en: Revealed in Medina. Addresses social ethics — prohibiting suspicion, spying, backbiting, and establishing racial equality among believers.
s_ms: Al-Hujurat. Adab di rumah tangga Nabi, persaudaraan orang beriman, dan bahaya mengejek dan mengumpat.
h_ms: Diturunkan di Madinah. Menangani etika sosial — melarang sangkaan buruk, mengintip, mengumpat, dan menubuhkan kesamarataan kaum dalam kalangan orang beriman.

s_en: Qaf. Affirms resurrection through Allah's power of creation and the ever-present recording angels.
h_en: Revealed in Mecca. Opens with the letter 'Qaf' and powerfully argues for bodily resurrection using the evidence of Allah's creative power.
s_ms: Qaf. Menegaskan kebangkitan melalui kuasa penciptaan Allah dan malaikat pencatat yang sentiasa hadir.
h_ms: Diturunkan di Makkah. Bermula dengan huruf 'Qaf' dan berhujah dengan kuat tentang kebangkitan jasad menggunakan bukti kuasa penciptaan Allah.

s_en: The Scatterers. Oaths by the winds, affirming resurrection, and stories of Ibrahim and other prophets.
h_en: Revealed in Mecca. Uses powerful oaths by natural phenomena to affirm the reality of Judgment Day and divine provision.
s_ms: Az-Zariyat. Sumpah demi angin, menegaskan kebangkitan, dan kisah Ibrahim serta nabi-nabi lain.
h_ms: Diturunkan di Makkah. Menggunakan sumpah yang kuat demi fenomena alam untuk menegaskan kenyataan Hari Kiamat dan rezeki ilahi.

s_en: The Mount. Oaths by Mount Sinai, affirming the Quran's truth and the punishment awaiting deniers.
h_en: Revealed in Mecca. Swears by Mount Tur (Sinai) where Musa received the Torah, connecting the Quran to earlier revelations.
s_ms: At-Tur. Sumpah demi Bukit Sinai, menegaskan kebenaran al-Quran dan azab yang menanti golongan yang mengingkari.
h_ms: Diturunkan di Makkah. Bersumpah demi Bukit Tur (Sinai) di mana Musa menerima Taurat, menghubungkan al-Quran dengan wahyu-wahyu terdahulu.

s_en: The Star. The Prophet's ﷺ ascension vision, refutation of idolatry, and the story of Ibrahim and Musa.
h_en: Revealed in Mecca. Describes the Prophet's ﷺ vision during the Mi'raj and was the first surah where the Prophet prostrated upon reciting publicly.
s_ms: An-Najm. Visi Mi'raj Nabi ﷺ, penolakan penyembahan berhala, dan kisah Ibrahim dan Musa.
h_ms: Diturunkan di Makkah. Menggambarkan visi Nabi ﷺ semasa Mi'raj dan merupakan surah pertama di mana Nabi sujud semasa membaca secara terbuka.

s_en: The Moon. The splitting of the moon as a miracle, and stories of destroyed nations who rejected their prophets.
h_en: Revealed in Mecca. Opens with the splitting of the moon — a miracle witnessed by the Quraysh — and the refrain 'How terrible were My punishment and My warnings.'
s_ms: Al-Qamar. Pembelahan bulan sebagai mukjizat, dan kisah-kisah umat yang binasa kerana menolak nabi mereka.
h_ms: Diturunkan di Makkah. Bermula dengan pembelahan bulan — mukjizat yang disaksikan oleh Quraisy — dan ungkapan berulang 'Betapa dahsyatnya azab-Ku dan amaran-Ku.'

s_en: The Most Merciful. A lyrical celebration of Allah's blessings, repeatedly asking 'Which of the favors of your Lord will you deny?'
h_en: Revealed in Medina (according to many scholars). Known for its unique rhythmic style and the famous refrain repeated 31 times.
s_ms: Ar-Rahman. Pujian puitis atas nikmat Allah, berulang kali bertanya 'Nikmat Tuhan kamu yang manakah yang kamu dustakan?'
h_ms: Diturunkan di Madinah (menurut kebanyakan ulama). Dikenali dengan gaya berirama yang unik dan ungkapan masyhur yang diulang 31 kali.

s_en: The Inevitable. Describes three groups on Judgment Day: the Foremost, the Right, and the Left — and their respective fates.
h_en: Revealed in Mecca. Vividly categorizes humanity into three groups based on their deeds, describing Paradise and Hellfire in striking detail.
s_ms: Al-Waqi'ah. Menggambarkan tiga kumpulan pada Hari Kiamat: As-Sabiqun, Ashabul Yamin, dan Ashabusy Syimal — dan nasib masing-masing.
h_ms: Diturunkan di Makkah. Dengan jelas mengkategorikan manusia kepada tiga kumpulan berdasarkan amal mereka, menggambarkan Syurga dan Neraka dengan terperinci.

s_en: The Iron. All creation glorifies Allah; the importance of spending in His cause and the reality of worldly life.
h_en: Revealed in Medina. Named for iron's divinely bestowed strength and the call to spend in Allah's cause before death approaches.
s_ms: Al-Hadid. Semua makhluk bertasbih kepada Allah; kepentingan berinfak di jalan-Nya dan hakikat kehidupan dunia.
h_ms: Diturunkan di Madinah. Dinamakan kerana kekuatan besi yang dikurniakan Allah dan seruan untuk berinfak di jalan Allah sebelum kematian mendekati.

s_en: The Pleading Woman. The story of Khawlah who pleaded to the Prophet ﷺ, and rulings on zihar (a pre-Islamic divorce practice).
h_en: Revealed in Medina. Khawlah bint Tha'labah complained to the Prophet ﷺ about her husband's zihar oath, and Allah responded directly.
s_ms: Al-Mujadalah. Kisah Khaulah yang mengadu kepada Nabi ﷺ, dan hukum zihar (amalan cerai zaman Jahiliyah).
h_ms: Diturunkan di Madinah. Khaulah binti Tha'labah mengadu kepada Nabi ﷺ tentang sumpah zihar suaminya, dan Allah menjawab secara langsung.

s_en: The Gathering. The expulsion of the treacherous Banu Nadir tribe and rules for distributing community wealth.
h_en: Revealed in Medina after the expulsion of the Jewish tribe of Banu Nadir (4 AH) for their treachery and treaty violation.
s_ms: Al-Hasyr. Pengusiran kabilah Bani Nadir yang khianat dan peraturan pembahagian harta masyarakat.
h_ms: Diturunkan di Madinah selepas pengusiran kabilah Yahudi Bani Nadir (4 H) kerana pengkhianatan dan pelanggaran perjanjian mereka.

s_en: The Woman Examined. Tests of faith for emigrant women, alliances with non-Muslims, and the oath of allegiance for women.
h_en: Revealed in Medina around the Treaty of Hudaybiyyah (6 AH), addressing the examination of women who emigrated to Medina.
s_ms: Al-Mumtahanah. Ujian iman bagi wanita Muhajirin, hubungan dengan bukan Islam, dan bai'ah wanita.
h_ms: Diturunkan di Madinah sekitar Perjanjian Hudaibiyah (6 H), menangani pemeriksaan wanita yang berhijrah ke Madinah.

s_en: The Row. The importance of fighting in Allah's cause in organized ranks and the prophecy of eventual victory.
h_en: Revealed in Medina. Emphasizes unity and discipline in the Muslim community, comparing believers to a solid, well-ordered structure.
s_ms: As-Saff. Kepentingan berjuang di jalan Allah dalam barisan yang teratur dan ramalan kemenangan akhirnya.
h_ms: Diturunkan di Madinah. Menekankan perpaduan dan disiplin dalam masyarakat Islam, menyamakan orang beriman dengan struktur yang kukuh dan teratur.

s_en: Friday. The obligation of Jumu'ah prayer and a rebuke for leaving the Prophet ﷺ during a sermon for trade.
h_en: Revealed in Medina. Establishes the Friday congregational prayer and recounts when some companions left the sermon for a trade caravan.
s_ms: Al-Jumu'ah. Kewajipan solat Jumaat dan teguran kerana meninggalkan Nabi ﷺ semasa khutbah demi perniagaan.
h_ms: Diturunkan di Madinah. Menetapkan solat Jumaat berjemaah dan menceritakan apabila sebahagian sahabat meninggalkan khutbah demi kafilah dagang.

s_en: The Hypocrites. Exposes the hypocrites who secretly undermined the Muslim community from within.
h_en: Revealed in Medina after the Battle of Banu Mustaliq (6 AH), when the hypocrite Abdullah ibn Ubayy made threatening statements.
s_ms: Al-Munafiqun. Membongkar golongan munafik yang secara rahsia melemahkan masyarakat Islam dari dalam.
h_ms: Diturunkan di Madinah selepas Perang Bani Mustaliq (6 H), apabila Abdullah bin Ubayy yang munafik membuat kenyataan mengancam.

s_en: Mutual Disillusion. The Day of Judgment when all illusions of worldly power will be stripped away.
h_en: Revealed in Medina. Named for the Day when people's self-deception will be fully exposed and mutual loss will be evident.
s_ms: At-Taghabun. Hari Kiamat apabila semua ilusi kuasa duniawi akan terungkai.
h_ms: Diturunkan di Madinah. Dinamakan sempena Hari di mana penipuan diri sendiri akan terdedah sepenuhnya dan kerugian bersama akan nyata.

s_en: Divorce. Laws governing divorce, waiting periods ('iddah), and the maintenance of divorced women.
h_en: Revealed in Medina. Provides detailed legislation on divorce procedures, reflecting Islam's concern for justice even in separation.
s_ms: At-Talaq. Hukum-hukum perceraian, tempoh iddah, dan nafkah wanita yang diceraikan.
h_ms: Diturunkan di Madinah. Memberikan perundangan terperinci tentang prosedur perceraian, mencerminkan keprihatinan Islam terhadap keadilan walaupun dalam perpisahan.

s_en: The Prohibition. The Prophet ﷺ is admonished for prohibiting what Allah made lawful; lessons from the wives of Nuh and Lut.
h_en: Revealed in Medina. Addresses an incident where the Prophet ﷺ prohibited honey for himself, and warns against alliances that undermine faith.
s_ms: At-Tahrim. Nabi ﷺ ditegur kerana mengharamkan apa yang Allah halalkan; pengajaran daripada isteri Nuh dan Lut.
h_ms: Diturunkan di Madinah. Menangani peristiwa apabila Nabi ﷺ mengharamkan madu untuk dirinya, dan memberi amaran terhadap pakatan yang melemahkan iman.

s_en: The Sovereignty. Allah's dominion over life and death, and the purpose of creation as a test of deeds.
h_en: Revealed in Mecca. The Prophet ﷺ said whoever recites it every night will be protected from the punishment of the grave.
s_ms: Al-Mulk. Kedaulatan Allah atas kehidupan dan kematian, dan tujuan penciptaan sebagai ujian amal.
h_ms: Diturunkan di Makkah. Nabi ﷺ bersabda sesiapa yang membacanya setiap malam akan dilindungi daripada azab kubur.

s_en: The Pen. Defends the Prophet's ﷺ sanity and character, and warns against the arrogance of the wealthy.
h_en: Revealed in Mecca. One of the earliest revelations, comforting the Prophet ﷺ against accusations of madness by the Quraysh.
s_ms: Al-Qalam. Membela kewarasan dan akhlak Nabi ﷺ, dan memberi amaran terhadap kesombongan orang kaya.
h_ms: Diturunkan di Makkah. Antara wahyu terawal, menghiburkan Nabi ﷺ terhadap tuduhan gila oleh Quraisy.

s_en: The Inevitable Reality. Vivid descriptions of Judgment Day and the fates of the people of the right and left hands.
h_en: Revealed in Mecca. Named 'Al-Haqqah' (The Inevitable Reality) — a name for Judgment Day that emphasizes its absolute certainty.
s_ms: Al-Haqqah. Gambaran jelas tentang Hari Kiamat dan nasib golongan kanan dan kiri.
h_ms: Diturunkan di Makkah. Dinamakan 'Al-Haqqah' (Hari Yang Pasti) — nama untuk Hari Kiamat yang menekankan kepastiannya yang mutlak.

s_en: The Ways of Ascent. Describes a man's plea for punishment to hasten, and the qualities of those who will be saved.
h_en: Revealed in Mecca. Responds to a disbeliever who challenged the Prophet ﷺ to bring punishment sooner, describing the spiritual ascent to Allah.
s_ms: Al-Ma'arij. Menggambarkan rayuan seseorang agar azab disegerakan, dan sifat-sifat mereka yang akan diselamatkan.
h_ms: Diturunkan di Makkah. Menjawab orang kafir yang mencabar Nabi ﷺ untuk mendatangkan azab lebih cepat, menggambarkan kenaikan rohani ke hadrat Allah.

s_en: Noah. The complete account of Nuh's 950-year mission, his people's rejection, and the great flood.
h_en: Revealed in Mecca. Parallels the Prophet's ﷺ own experience of prolonged rejection, offering consolation through Nuh's perseverance.
s_ms: Nuh. Kisah lengkap dakwah Nuh selama 950 tahun, penolakan kaumnya, dan banjir besar.
h_ms: Diturunkan di Makkah. Menyamakan pengalaman Nabi ﷺ sendiri dalam penolakan yang berpanjangan, memberikan penghiburan melalui ketabahan Nuh.

s_en: The Jinn. The jinn's response to hearing the Quran and their acceptance of Islam.
h_en: Revealed in Mecca. Narrates how a group of jinn heard the Prophet ﷺ reciting the Quran and embraced Islam, spreading the message to their kind.
s_ms: Al-Jinn. Respons jin apabila mendengar al-Quran dan penerimaan mereka terhadap Islam.
h_ms: Diturunkan di Makkah. Menceritakan bagaimana sekumpulan jin mendengar Nabi ﷺ membaca al-Quran dan memeluk Islam, menyebarkan mesej kepada golongan mereka.

s_en: The Enwrapped One. Commands the Prophet ﷺ to arise and warn, establishing night prayer (Qiyam al-Layl).
h_en: One of the earliest revelations in Mecca. The Prophet ﷺ came home trembling after his first encounter with Jibril, and was told to arise and warn.
s_ms: Al-Muzzammil. Memerintahkan Nabi ﷺ bangkit dan memberi amaran, menetapkan solat malam (Qiamulail).
h_ms: Antara wahyu terawal di Makkah. Nabi ﷺ pulang dalam keadaan menggeletar selepas pertemuan pertama dengan Jibril, lalu diperintahkan bangkit dan memberi amaran.

s_en: The Cloaked One. The call to arise and warn, the blowing of the trumpet, and personal responsibility for one's soul.
h_en: Revealed in Mecca shortly after Al-Muzzammil. Addresses the Prophet ﷺ as 'the cloaked one' and commands public proclamation of the message.
s_ms: Al-Muddasir. Seruan untuk bangkit dan memberi amaran, tiupan sangkakala, dan tanggungjawab peribadi terhadap jiwa sendiri.
h_ms: Diturunkan di Makkah sejurus selepas Al-Muzzammil. Menyeru Nabi ﷺ sebagai 'yang berselimut' dan memerintahkan penyampaian risalah secara terbuka.

s_en: The Resurrection. A vivid depiction of the moment of death and resurrection, affirming Allah's power to rebuild humanity.
h_en: Revealed in Mecca. Addresses the skeptics who ask 'When is the Day of Resurrection?' with powerful imagery of the soul's final moments.
s_ms: Al-Qiamah. Gambaran jelas tentang saat kematian dan kebangkitan, menegaskan kuasa Allah untuk membina semula manusia.
h_ms: Diturunkan di Makkah. Menjawab golongan skeptik yang bertanya 'Bilakah Hari Kebangkitan?' dengan gambaran kuat tentang saat-saat akhir jiwa.

s_en: Man. Describes the creation of man, the rewards of Paradise, and the virtue of feeding the poor, orphan, and captive.
h_en: Revealed in Medina. Inspired by the vow of Ali and Fatimah to fast three days and give away their food to those in need.
s_ms: Al-Insan. Menerangkan penciptaan manusia, ganjaran Syurga, dan kelebihan memberi makan kepada fakir, anak yatim, dan tawanan.
h_ms: Diturunkan di Madinah. Diilhamkan oleh nazar Ali dan Fatimah untuk berpuasa tiga hari dan memberikan makanan mereka kepada yang memerlukan.

s_en: Those Sent Forth. Oaths by the angels sent forth, affirming the Day of Judgment and woe to the deniers.
h_en: Revealed in Mecca. Uses a series of powerful oaths by winds and angels to affirm the certainty of resurrection and judgment.
s_ms: Al-Mursalat. Sumpah demi malaikat yang diutus, menegaskan Hari Kiamat dan kecelaan bagi golongan yang mengingkari.
h_ms: Diturunkan di Makkah. Menggunakan siri sumpah yang kuat demi angin dan malaikat untuk menegaskan kepastian kebangkitan dan pengadilan.

s_en: The Announcement. A dialogue between the people of Paradise and Hellfire about the Great Announcement of resurrection.
h_en: Revealed in Mecca. Named for the 'Great Announcement' — the resurrection — which the disbelievers questioned and debated among themselves.
s_ms: An-Naba'. Dialog antara penduduk Syurga dan Neraka tentang Berita Besar iaitu kebangkitan.
h_ms: Diturunkan di Makkah. Dinamakan sempena 'Berita Besar' — kebangkitan — yang dipersoalkan dan diperdebatkan oleh orang kafir dalam kalangan mereka.

s_en: Those Who Drag Forth. Oaths by the angels who extract souls, describing the terror and transformation of Judgment Day.
h_en: Revealed in Mecca. Describes the angels who forcefully extract souls of the wicked, contrasting with those who gently take the souls of the righteous.
s_ms: An-Nazi'at. Sumpah demi malaikat yang mencabut nyawa, menggambarkan kedahsyatan dan transformasi Hari Kiamat.
h_ms: Diturunkan di Makkah. Menggambarkan malaikat yang mencabut nyawa orang jahat dengan keras, berbeza dengan yang mencabut nyawa orang soleh dengan lembut.

s_en: He Frowned. The Prophet ﷺ is gently rebuked for turning away from a blind man seeking knowledge.
h_en: Revealed in Mecca. Abdullah ibn Umm Maktum, a blind companion, approached the Prophet ﷺ while he was engaged with Quraysh leaders.
s_ms: 'Abasa. Nabi ﷺ ditegur dengan lembut kerana berpaling daripada seorang buta yang menuntut ilmu.
h_ms: Diturunkan di Makkah. Abdullah bin Ummi Maktum, seorang sahabat yang buta, mendekati Nabi ﷺ semasa Baginda sedang berbincang dengan pemimpin Quraisy.

s_en: The Overthrowing. Striking imagery of cosmic upheaval on Judgment Day — the sun darkened, mountains moved, souls paired.
h_en: Revealed in Mecca. One of the earliest surahs, its vivid imagery of cosmic destruction moved listeners and drew some to Islam.
s_ms: At-Takwir. Gambaran jelas tentang pergolakan kosmik pada Hari Kiamat — matahari digelapkan, gunung dipindahkan, jiwa dipasangkan.
h_ms: Diturunkan di Makkah. Antara surah terawal, gambaran jelas tentang kemusnahan kosmik menggerakkan hati pendengar dan menarik sebahagian mereka kepada Islam.

s_en: The Cleaving. The sky splitting apart, angels recording deeds, and the contrasting fates of the righteous and wicked.
h_en: Revealed in Mecca. Describes the sky cleaving open on Judgment Day and the record of deeds being laid bare.
s_ms: Al-Infitar. Langit terbelah, malaikat mencatat amal, dan nasib berbeza antara orang soleh dan jahat.
h_ms: Diturunkan di Makkah. Menggambarkan langit terbelah pada Hari Kiamat dan catatan amal dibentangkan.

s_en: Those Who Give Short Measure. Condemnation of fraud in trade and the record of deeds kept in Sijjin and Illiyyun.
h_en: Revealed in Mecca. Addresses dishonest merchants who cheat in measurements, linking commercial fraud to denial of Judgment Day.
s_ms: Al-Mutaffifin. Kecaman terhadap penipuan dalam perniagaan dan catatan amal yang disimpan di Sijjin dan Illiyyun.
h_ms: Diturunkan di Makkah. Menangani pedagang yang tidak jujur yang menipu dalam sukatan, mengaitkan penipuan perniagaan dengan pengingkaran Hari Kiamat.

s_en: The Splitting Asunder. The sky splitting, the earth spreading, and humanity's journey toward their Lord.
h_en: Revealed in Mecca. Contains a verse of prostration and portrays the cosmic transformation when the heavens and earth obey Allah's command.
s_ms: Al-Insyiqaq. Langit terbelah, bumi terbentang, dan perjalanan manusia menuju Tuhan mereka.
h_ms: Diturunkan di Makkah. Mengandungi ayat sujud dan menggambarkan transformasi kosmik apabila langit dan bumi mematuhi perintah Allah.

s_en: The Great Stars. The story of the boy and the tyrant king, the People of the Trench, and Allah's power over creation.
h_en: Revealed in Mecca. Contains the parable of the believers thrown into a fiery trench for their faith, illustrating the cost of conviction.
s_ms: Al-Buruj. Kisah anak muda dan raja zalim, Ashabul Ukhdud, dan kuasa Allah ke atas penciptaan.
h_ms: Diturunkan di Makkah. Mengandungi perumpamaan orang beriman yang dicampak ke dalam parit berapi kerana iman mereka, menggambarkan harga keyakinan.

s_en: The Night-Comer. Oaths by the night-visitor star, affirming that every soul has a guardian and resurrection is real.
h_en: Revealed in Mecca. Named after the star that appears at night, using it as an oath to affirm Allah's watchful presence over every soul.
s_ms: At-Tariq. Sumpah demi bintang yang datang pada waktu malam, menegaskan bahawa setiap jiwa mempunyai penjaga dan kebangkitan itu benar.
h_ms: Diturunkan di Makkah. Dinamakan sempena bintang yang muncul pada waktu malam, menggunakannya sebagai sumpah untuk menegaskan kehadiran Allah yang mengawasi setiap jiwa.

s_en: The Most High. Glorification of Allah, the success of the purified, and the preference for the Hereafter over worldly life.
h_en: Revealed in Mecca. One of the earliest surahs, frequently recited by the Prophet ﷺ in the first rak'ah of Jumu'ah and Eid prayers.
s_ms: Al-A'la. Pemujaan terhadap Allah, kejayaan orang yang menyucikan diri, dan keutamaan akhirat berbanding dunia.
h_ms: Diturunkan di Makkah. Antara surah terawal, sering dibaca oleh Nabi ﷺ dalam rakaat pertama solat Jumaat dan Hari Raya.

s_en: The Overwhelming. Contrasts the faces of the punished and the blessed on Judgment Day, calling people to reflect.
h_en: Revealed in Mecca. The Prophet ﷺ frequently paired it with Al-Ghashiyah in Jumu'ah prayers, describing the two contrasting fates.
s_ms: Al-Ghasyiyah. Membezakan wajah orang yang diazab dan yang dirahmati pada Hari Kiamat, menyeru manusia untuk berfikir.
h_ms: Diturunkan di Makkah. Nabi ﷺ sering menggabungkannya dalam solat Jumaat, menggambarkan dua nasib yang berbeza.

s_en: The Dawn. Oaths by the dawn and the ten nights, the test of wealth and poverty, and the call to the contented soul.
h_en: Revealed in Mecca. References the ten nights (likely of Dhul-Hijjah) and ends with a beautiful address to the 'contented soul' entering Paradise.
s_ms: Al-Fajr. Sumpah demi fajar dan sepuluh malam, ujian kekayaan dan kemiskinan, dan seruan kepada jiwa yang tenang.
h_ms: Diturunkan di Makkah. Merujuk kepada sepuluh malam (kemungkinan Zulhijjah) dan berakhir dengan alamat indah kepada 'jiwa yang tenang' memasuki Syurga.

s_en: The City. Oaths by the city of Makkah, defining true righteousness as freeing slaves and feeding the hungry.
h_en: Revealed in Mecca. Defines the 'steep path' of righteousness as concrete acts of compassion — freeing slaves, feeding orphans and the needy.
s_ms: Al-Balad. Sumpah demi kota Makkah, mentakrifkan kebajikan sejati sebagai membebaskan hamba dan memberi makan kepada yang lapar.
h_ms: Diturunkan di Makkah. Mentakrifkan 'jalan yang curam' kebajikan sebagai amal kasih sayang yang nyata — membebaskan hamba, memberi makan anak yatim dan orang yang memerlukan.

s_en: The Sun. Oaths by seven cosmic phenomena, affirming that success comes from purifying the soul and failure from corrupting it.
h_en: Revealed in Mecca. Opens with the most consecutive oaths in the Quran (7), all pointing to one truth: the soul's success depends on its purification.
s_ms: Asy-Syams. Sumpah demi tujuh fenomena kosmik, menegaskan kejayaan datang daripada menyucikan jiwa dan kegagalan daripada mengotori.
h_ms: Diturunkan di Makkah. Bermula dengan sumpah berturutan paling banyak dalam al-Quran (7), semuanya menunjuk kepada satu kebenaran: kejayaan jiwa bergantung pada penyuciannya.

s_en: The Night. Contrasts the one who gives in charity with the one who hoards, and the different paths they walk.
h_en: Revealed in Mecca. Contrasts two types of people — the generous believer and the stingy disbeliever — and their ultimate destinations.
s_ms: Al-Lail. Membezakan orang yang bersedekah dengan yang bakhil, dan jalan berbeza yang mereka lalui.
h_ms: Diturunkan di Makkah. Membezakan dua jenis manusia — orang beriman yang pemurah dan orang kafir yang bakhil — dan destinasi akhir mereka.

s_en: The Morning Brightness. A tender reassurance to the Prophet ﷺ that Allah has not abandoned him, even in silence.
h_en: Revealed in Mecca after a pause in revelation. The Prophet ﷺ was distressed, and Allah comforted him, reminding him of past blessings.
s_ms: Ad-Duha. Jaminan lembut kepada Nabi ﷺ bahawa Allah tidak meninggalkannya, walaupun dalam kesunyian.
h_ms: Diturunkan di Makkah selepas tempoh tiada wahyu. Nabi ﷺ berasa sedih, lalu Allah menghiburkannya, mengingatkan Baginda tentang nikmat-nikmat lalu.

s_en: The Opening Forth. Allah expanded the Prophet's ﷺ chest and relieved his burden; with hardship comes ease.
h_en: Revealed in Mecca, closely linked to Ad-Duha. Contains the beloved promise 'With hardship comes ease' — repeated twice for emphasis.
s_ms: Asy-Syarh. Allah melapangkan dada Nabi ﷺ dan meringankan bebannya; bersama kesulitan ada kemudahan.
h_ms: Diturunkan di Makkah, berkait rapat dengan Ad-Duha. Mengandungi janji yang dicintai 'Bersama kesulitan ada kemudahan' — diulang dua kali untuk penekanan.

s_en: The Fig. Oaths by sacred places, affirming man's noble creation and his potential fall to the lowest of the low.
h_en: Revealed in Mecca. Swears by the fig, olive, Mount Sinai, and Makkah to affirm that man was created in the best form but can fall through disbelief.
s_ms: At-Tin. Sumpah demi tempat-tempat suci, menegaskan penciptaan mulia manusia dan potensi kejatuhannya ke tempat yang paling rendah.
h_ms: Diturunkan di Makkah. Bersumpah demi tin, zaitun, Bukit Sinai, dan Makkah untuk menegaskan bahawa manusia dicipta dalam bentuk terbaik tetapi boleh jatuh melalui kekafiran.

s_en: The Clot. The first revelation to the Prophet ﷺ — 'Read in the name of your Lord' — marking the dawn of prophethood.
h_en: The very first verses revealed to the Prophet ﷺ in the Cave of Hira during Ramadan 610 CE, through the Angel Jibril.
s_ms: Al-'Alaq. Wahyu pertama kepada Nabi ﷺ — 'Bacalah dengan nama Tuhanmu' — menandakan permulaan kenabian.
h_ms: Ayat-ayat yang pertama sekali diturunkan kepada Nabi ﷺ di Gua Hira' pada bulan Ramadan 610 M, melalui Malaikat Jibril.

s_en: The Night of Decree. Describes Laylat al-Qadr — a night better than a thousand months — when the Quran was first sent down.
h_en: Revealed in Mecca. Highlights the blessed night when the Quran descended to the lowest heaven, and angels descend with peace until dawn.
s_ms: Al-Qadr. Menerangkan Lailatul Qadr — malam yang lebih baik daripada seribu bulan — apabila al-Quran pertama kali diturunkan.
h_ms: Diturunkan di Makkah. Menekankan malam yang berkat apabila al-Quran diturunkan ke langit dunia, dan malaikat turun membawa keamanan sehingga fajar.

s_en: The Clear Evidence. The People of the Book and polytheists are distinguished by their response to the clear message.
h_en: Revealed in Medina. Addresses the People of the Book who, despite clear evidence in their scriptures, rejected the final Messenger.
s_ms: Al-Bayyinah. Ahli Kitab dan kaum musyrikin dibezakan oleh respons mereka terhadap mesej yang jelas.
h_ms: Diturunkan di Madinah. Menangani Ahli Kitab yang, walaupun terdapat bukti yang jelas dalam kitab mereka, menolak Rasul terakhir.

s_en: The Earthquake. A brief but powerful description of the earth shaking and revealing all that humanity has done.
h_en: Revealed in Medina. The Prophet ﷺ said whoever recites it will be as if they recited the entire Quran, due to its comprehensive depiction of Judgment.
s_ms: Az-Zalzalah. Penerangan ringkas tetapi kuat tentang bumi yang bergoncang dan mendedahkan semua yang manusia telah lakukan.
h_ms: Diturunkan di Madinah. Nabi ﷺ bersabda sesiapa yang membacanya seolah-olah telah membaca keseluruhan al-Quran, kerana gambaran menyeluruhnya tentang Hari Pengadilan.

s_en: The Racers. Oaths by war-horses charging at dawn, revealing humanity's ingratitude and love of wealth.
h_en: Revealed in Mecca. Uses vivid imagery of charging horses to expose human ingratitude, ending with the reminder of resurrection.
s_ms: Al-'Adiyat. Sumpah demi kuda perang yang menyerbu pada waktu subuh, mendedahkan ketidakbersyukuran manusia dan kecintaan terhadap harta.
h_ms: Diturunkan di Makkah. Menggunakan gambaran jelas kuda yang menyerbu untuk mendedahkan ketidakbersyukuran manusia, berakhir dengan peringatan tentang kebangkitan.

s_en: The Striking Calamity. The Day of Judgment when people scatter like moths and mountains become like carded wool.
h_en: Revealed in Mecca. Describes the striking catastrophe of Judgment Day with vivid metaphors — people like scattered moths, mountains like fluffed wool.
s_ms: Al-Qari'ah. Hari Kiamat apabila manusia berterbangan seperti kelkatu dan gunung menjadi seperti bulu yang dihamburkan.
h_ms: Diturunkan di Makkah. Menggambarkan bencana dahsyat Hari Kiamat dengan metafora yang jelas — manusia seperti kelkatu yang berterbangan, gunung seperti bulu yang dihamburkan.

s_en: Rivalry in Worldly Increase. The distraction of worldly competition until death, and the certainty of being questioned.
h_en: Revealed in Mecca. A stern warning against the obsession with accumulating wealth and status, which distracts from life's true purpose.
s_ms: At-Takasur. Kelalaian persaingan duniawi sehingga mati, dan kepastian akan dipersoalkan.
h_ms: Diturunkan di Makkah. Amaran keras terhadap obsesi mengumpul kekayaan dan status, yang melalaikan daripada tujuan sebenar kehidupan.

s_en: The Time. A concise declaration that all humanity is in loss — except those with faith, good deeds, truth, and patience.
h_en: Revealed in Mecca. Imam Shafi'i said if people reflected on this surah alone, it would suffice them as guidance for life.
s_ms: Al-'Asr. Perisytiharan ringkas bahawa seluruh manusia berada dalam kerugian — kecuali mereka yang beriman, beramal soleh, berpesan dengan kebenaran, dan bersabar.
h_ms: Diturunkan di Makkah. Imam Syafi'i berkata jika manusia merenungi surah ini sahaja, ia sudah memadai sebagai panduan hidup.

s_en: The Slanderer. Condemnation of backbiters and slanderers, warning of the crushing fire (Hutamah).
h_en: Revealed in Mecca. Targets those who mock others, hoard wealth, and slander reputations — warning of a fire that reaches the hearts.
s_ms: Al-Humazah. Kecaman terhadap pengumpat dan pemfitnah, amaran tentang api yang menghancurkan (Hutamah).
h_ms: Diturunkan di Makkah. Mensasarkan mereka yang mengejek orang lain, menimbun harta, dan memfitnah — memberi amaran tentang api yang sampai ke hati.

s_en: The Elephant. The miraculous destruction of Abraha's army of elephants that marched to destroy the Ka'bah.
h_en: Revealed in Mecca. Recounts the event of 570 CE (Year of the Elephant, also the Prophet's ﷺ birth year) when Allah sent flocks of birds to destroy Abraha's army.
s_ms: Al-Fil. Kemusnahan mukjizat tentera gajah Abrahah yang berarak untuk memusnahkan Ka'bah.
h_ms: Diturunkan di Makkah. Menceritakan peristiwa 570 M (Tahun Gajah, juga tahun kelahiran Nabi ﷺ) apabila Allah menghantar kawanan burung untuk memusnahkan tentera Abrahah.

s_en: Quraysh. A brief reminder to the Quraysh tribe to worship the Lord of the Ka'bah who granted them safety and provision.
h_en: Revealed in Mecca. Connected to Al-Fil, it reminds the Quraysh that their prosperity and safety are blessings from Allah, deserving of gratitude.
s_ms: Quraisy. Peringatan ringkas kepada kabilah Quraisy untuk menyembah Tuhan Ka'bah yang memberikan mereka keselamatan dan rezeki.
h_ms: Diturunkan di Makkah. Berkaitan dengan Al-Fil, ia mengingatkan Quraisy bahawa kemakmuran dan keselamatan mereka adalah nikmat Allah yang layak disyukuri.

s_en: Small Kindnesses. Condemns those who pray for show yet neglect the orphan and refuse small acts of kindness.
h_en: Revealed in Mecca. Defines the 'denier of religion' not as one who disbelieves, but one who neglects the vulnerable and withholds basic kindness.
s_ms: Al-Ma'un. Mengecam mereka yang solat untuk menunjuk tetapi mengabaikan anak yatim dan enggan melakukan kebaikan kecil.
h_ms: Diturunkan di Makkah. Mentakrifkan 'pendusta agama' bukan sebagai orang yang kafir, tetapi yang mengabaikan golongan rentan dan menghalang kebaikan asas.

s_en: The Abundance. Allah granted the Prophet ﷺ abundant goodness (Al-Kawthar — a river in Paradise), so he should pray and sacrifice.
h_en: Revealed in Mecca when Al-'As ibn Wa'il mocked the Prophet ﷺ for having no surviving sons, calling him 'abtar' (cut off).
s_ms: Al-Kausar. Allah mengurniakan Nabi ﷺ kebaikan yang banyak (Al-Kausar — sungai di Syurga), maka hendaklah Baginda solat dan berkorban.
h_ms: Diturunkan di Makkah apabila Al-'As bin Wa'il mengejek Nabi ﷺ kerana tiada anak lelaki yang hidup, memanggil Baginda 'abtar' (terputus).

s_en: The Disbelievers. A firm declaration of religious freedom — 'For you is your religion, and for me is mine.'
h_en: Revealed in Mecca. The Quraysh proposed a compromise: worship their idols one year, they would worship Allah the next. This surah firmly rejected the offer.
s_ms: Al-Kafirun. Perisytiharan tegas tentang kebebasan beragama — 'Untukmu agamamu, dan untukku agamaku.'
h_ms: Diturunkan di Makkah. Quraisy mencadangkan kompromi: sembah berhala mereka setahun, mereka akan menyembah Allah tahun berikutnya. Surah ini dengan tegas menolak tawaran itu.

s_en: The Help. Foretells the conquest of Makkah and people entering Islam in multitudes; a command to seek forgiveness.
h_en: One of the last surahs revealed, signaling the approaching end of the Prophet's ﷺ mission and the triumph of Islam in Arabia.
s_ms: An-Nasr. Meramalkan penaklukan Makkah dan orang ramai memasuki Islam secara berbondong-bondong; perintah untuk beristighfar.
h_ms: Antara surah terakhir yang diturunkan, menandakan hampir berakhirnya misi Nabi ﷺ dan kemenangan Islam di Tanah Arab.

s_en: The Palm Fiber. Condemnation of Abu Lahab and his wife for their fierce opposition to the Prophet ﷺ.
h_en: Revealed in Mecca. Abu Lahab, the Prophet's ﷺ uncle, publicly opposed him. His wife carried thorns to block the Prophet's path.
s_ms: Al-Masad. Kecaman terhadap Abu Lahab dan isterinya kerana penentangan sengit mereka terhadap Nabi ﷺ.
h_ms: Diturunkan di Makkah. Abu Lahab, bapa saudara Nabi ﷺ, menentang Baginda secara terbuka. Isterinya membawa duri untuk menghalang laluan Nabi.

s_en: Sincerity. A concise declaration of Allah's absolute oneness — He is One, Eternal, neither begets nor is begotten.
h_en: Revealed in Mecca. The Prophet ﷺ said it equals one-third of the Quran. It was revealed in response to the Quraysh asking 'Describe your Lord's lineage.'
s_ms: Al-Ikhlas. Perisytiharan ringkas tentang keesaan mutlak Allah — Dia Maha Esa, tempat bergantung, tidak beranak dan tidak diperanakkan.
h_ms: Diturunkan di Makkah. Nabi ﷺ bersabda ia menyamai satu pertiga al-Quran. Ia diturunkan sebagai jawapan kepada Quraisy yang bertanya 'Sifatkanlah keturunan Tuhanmu.'

s_en: The Daybreak. Seeking refuge in Allah from the evil of creation, darkness, witchcraft, and envy.
h_en: Revealed in Mecca. Along with An-Nas, these are the 'two protectors' — the Prophet ﷺ recited them nightly and for protection against harm.
s_ms: Al-Falaq. Memohon perlindungan Allah daripada kejahatan makhluk, kegelapan, sihir, dan hasad dengki.
h_ms: Diturunkan di Makkah. Bersama An-Nas, ia adalah 'dua pelindung' — Nabi ﷺ membacanya setiap malam dan untuk perlindungan daripada bahaya.

s_en: Mankind. Seeking refuge in Allah, the Lord and King of mankind, from the whisperer who whispers evil into hearts.
h_en: Revealed in Mecca. The final surah of the Quran, pairing with Al-Falaq as the ultimate refuge in Allah from all evil, external and internal.
s_ms: An-Nas. Memohon perlindungan Allah, Tuhan dan Raja manusia, daripada pembisik yang membisikkan kejahatan ke dalam hati.
h_ms: Diturunkan di Makkah. Surah terakhir al-Quran, berpasangan dengan Al-Falaq sebagai perlindungan mutlak dalam Allah daripada segala kejahatan, luaran dan dalaman.
